summary: The article supports a tiered context architecture with explicit routing as a concrete mechanism for managing agent context in one project, while keeping retrieval and generalization limits explicit.

bounded_claims.claim_text: The retrieval layer remained operationally limited because knowledge-base access used keyword substring matching rather than semantic retrieval.
bounded_claims.bounds: The system exposed retrieval tools, but retrieval depended on literal term overlap, so relevant context could be missed when task wording and stored documents did not share matching substrings; robust recall is not established.

bounded_claims.claim_text: The paper does not justify treating its context-to-code ratio or reported outcomes as general performance guidance beyond this single project.
bounded_claims.bounds: The 24.2% ratio is explicitly described as non-normative, and the evaluation is limited to one developer on one project without controlled experiments, so external validity remains narrow.
